revisedPrompt:
Create exactly one PNG image: a 64x64 pixel-art terrain tileset sheet for a 16-bit SNES-style top-down platformer/world-map asset. Profile: snes16_platformer_v1. Preset: world_forest_mass_16. Asset kind: terrain-tile. Asset type: tile. Canvas size exactly 64x64px, arranged as a 4 row x 4 column grid of 16 frames, each frame exactly 16x16px. Do not draw grid lines, borders, tile IDs, labels, UI, text, watermark, characters, logos, creatures, birds, weapons, armor, modern objects, or any recognizable protected-game likeness. Use crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, consistent palette, dark one-pixel outline where useful, top-left light. Fully opaque. Fill empty/non-tile space with solid #FF00FF magenta only, though the tiles should fill their cells edge-to-edge where appropriate. Every frame must remain contained inside its own 16x16 cell and keep consistent scale. Seamlessly tileable horizontally.

Subject: bright mythical-Japan satoyama top-down BAMBOO GROVE WALL (竹林) autotile, non-walkable background boundary element. Palette must be cooler and deeper green than a warm broadleaf forest: deep blue-green bamboo culms (竹色), dense blue-jade foliage, cool diffused back-light through canopy, bright cheerful atmosphere, not dark or gloomy. Think large geographic forest/bamboo region, not individual close-up tree sprites. The bamboo grove should read as an organic regional wall with depth and enclosure behind playable grass.

Culm style across all relevant tiles: tall vertical bamboo trunks rising bottom-to-top of each cell, slightly overlapping, rounded volume with bright left highlights, dark right shadows, soft reflected light at lower segments, visible inter-node rings spaced irregularly. Culm colors vary from pale yellow-green at young upper sections to deep blue-green older lower sections. Foliage: dense narrow lance-shaped bamboo leaves clustered in upper portions, tilted 5-15 degrees as if wind-drifted; alternate tilt direction between columns to avoid uniformity.

Strict 4x4 layout by cell position, left-to-right top-to-bottom:
Row 1, cells 1-4 CENTER interior: full-density bamboo wall tiles. Packed culms edge-to-edge, dense overhead leaf canopy, deep cool shade at base. Vary culm spacing and leaf cluster positions in each of the four cells.
Row 2, cells 5-8 DIRECTIONAL EDGES: N, S, W, E respectively. The named side shows bamboo-to-grass transition with a non-straight wavy boundary. Each edge must imply a 3-point high-low structure inside the 16x16 frame: bright rim/young shoots at boundary, dark vertical wall plane of tall dense culms behind, cool blue ground shadow fading onto open grass outside. Culms protrude irregularly into grass with scattered young shoots.
Row 3, cells 9-12 OUTER CORNERS: NW, NE, SW, SE respectively. Rounded organic corners where grove curves; culms splay outward around the corner, young shoots scatter into adjacent grass, no hard 90-degree edge.
Row 4, cells 13-16 ACCENT/TRANSITION: cell 13 thin single-column bamboo screen strip, one or two culms wide with semi-transparent narrow bamboo fence feeling; cell 14 bamboo-to-grass low bush/shoot transition with scattered young bamboo shoots and low fern among grass; cell 15 cast shadow overlay of bamboo culm silhouettes on open ground, cool dark stripe pattern for decoration layer beyond grove edge; cell 16 bamboo inner-corner concave tile, small open clearing within grove.

Tile quality: avoid visible checkerboard and repeated square stamps. Vary texture, edge breakup, lighting, culm spacing, foliage clusters, grass overlap, chipped edges, and decoration while keeping cohesive. Transitions should blend bamboo, grass, shadow, and road-like open ground naturally without hard rectangular bands. No isometric perspective. Output as crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with readable pixel clusters, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, and consistent identity and scale across all 16 frames.
